title: zephyr: create generic SCHC module
description: User-requested follow-up. Create a reusable generic Zephyr SCHC module rather than keeping SCHC only as a LICHEN-private subsystem. This is distinct from evaluating libSCHC: the module may wrap libSCHC if evaluation supports that, or may factor the existing lichen/subsys/lichen/schc implementation into a generic Zephyr module with LICHEN-specific rules/config layered on top. Acceptance: define module boundaries and public API for generic RFC 8724 SCHC compression/decompression and fragmentation; separate generic SCHC engine code from LICHEN rule tables and profile choices; provide Zephyr Kconfig/CMake integration usable by other apps; preserve existing LICHEN SCHC test vectors; document how LICHEN configures/installs its rule set; include at least one standalone sample or test target using the generic module without LICHEN-specific link code.
acceptance_criteria: After implementation, run the relevant tests/builds and then complete two separate code-review passes before closing. Each review pass should inspect the produced code against this Bead's acceptance criteria and file follow-up Beads for any discovered issues.
notes: Implemented generic Zephyr SCHC module under lichen/subsys/schc with public schc/schc.h API, Kconfig/CMake integration via CONFIG_SCHC, generic profile/rule dispatch, uncompressed fallback handling, and explicit fragmentation API boundary. LICHEN SCHC now defines a static lichen_schc_rules profile and routes lichen_schc_compress/decompress through schc_compress/decompress while preserving LICHEN validation, rule IDs, uncompressed fallback, and existing test-vector behavior. Added lichen/tests/schc_generic Zephyr ztest using CONFIG_SCHC without CONFIG_LICHEN, plus profile documentation in lichen/subsys/lichen/schc/README.md. Tests run: standalone SCHC vector test 12/12 passed; west build qemu_x86 lichen/tests/schc_generic passed; generic SCHC QEMU run passed 4/4 and printed PROJECT EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL before timeout cleanup; west build qemu_x86 lichen/tests/link_crypto passed; link_crypto QEMU run passed 2/2 and printed PROJECT EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL before timeout cleanup. Review pass 1 finding: zero-rule profile with uncompressed fallback returned invalid argument; fixed and added regression test. Review pass 2 findings: no blocking correctness issues in this scope; follow-ups created for generic bitstream helpers and full generic fragmentation engine.
close_reason: Completed generic SCHC module split, tests, and two review passes
